name: run_backtest
description: Run a backtest for long-only stock trading strategies on historical OHLCV CSV data. Use this to evaluate and compare strategy performance (SMA crossover, RSI mean reversion, or breakout) by computing win rate, total return, CAGR, max drawdown, Sharpe ratio, and trade-level logs. Supports transaction cost modeling via commission and slippage in basis points. Returns a JSON object with metrics, config, and trade history. Do not use this for live trading signals or as financial advice - backtests are research only and may overfit.

csv.description: Path to CSV file with Date and Close columns (Open/High/Low optional). Use an alias from scenario csv_aliases (e.g., 'sample') or an absolute/relative file path. Must contain at least 3 rows sorted by date ascending.

strategy.description: Strategy logic to use. 'sma-crossover' for trend-following (buy when fast SMA crosses above slow SMA). 'rsi-reversion' for mean reversion (buy when RSI falls below entry threshold, sell when it rises above exit threshold). 'breakout' for price momentum (buy on highs breakout, sell on lows breakdown).

fast-window.description: Fast simple moving average window in days. Only used when strategy is 'sma-crossover'. Must be positive and smaller than slow-window.

slow-window.description: Slow simple moving average window in days. Only used when strategy is 'sma-crossover'. Must be positive and greater than fast-window.

rsi-period.description: RSI calculation period. Only used when strategy is 'rsi-reversion'. Must be positive.

rsi-entry.description: RSI entry threshold (0-100). Buy when RSI drops below this value. Only used when strategy is 'rsi-reversion'. Must be in range [0, 100] and less than rsi-exit.

rsi-exit.description: RSI exit threshold (0-100). Sell when RSI rises above this value. Only used when strategy is 'rsi-reversion'. Must be in range [0, 100] and greater than rsi-entry.

lookback.description: Lookback period in days for breakout detection. Only used when strategy is 'breakout'. Must be greater than 1.

commission-bps.description: Commission rate in basis points (bps). Example: 5 bps = 0.05% per trade. Must be non-negative. Apply realistic costs (e.g., 5-10 bps for US equities).

slippage-bps.description: Slippage in basis points (bps). Example: 2 bps = 0.02% price impact per trade. Must be non-negative. Models market impact on execution.

initial-capital.description: Initial capital in same currency as CSV prices. Must be positive. Used as the starting portfolio value.

risk-free-rate.description: Annual risk-free rate as decimal (e.g., 0.02 = 2%). Used for Sharpe ratio calculation. Must be non-negative.

quiet.description: If true, suppress human-readable summary and return JSON output only. If false, print both summary and JSON.
